name: Dark Chocolate Pecan Torte
recipeInstructions:
Yield: 10 to 12 Servings
1. In a small microwave-safe bowl, place the baking chocolate and butter. Microwave on HIGH (100%) power for 1 minute or until smooth when stirred; cool slightly.
2. Line the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan with foil; butter the foil and sides of the pan. Pre-heat the oven to 350°F.
3. In a large mixing bowl, combine the chocolate mixture, sugar, and vanilla extract. Add the egg yolks, beating well after each addition. Blend in the flour and water, and beat until smooth. Stir in the pecans.
4. Clean the mixing beaters very well and, in a small bowl, beat the egg whites, cream of tartar, and salt until stiff peaks form. Carefully fold the egg whites into the chocolate mixture.
5. Pour the batter into the prepared pan. Bake for 45 minutes or until the top appears dry (The torte will not test done in the center).
6. Allow to cool for 1 hour before removing from the springform pan. Cover and refrigerate until firm.
7. Remove the side of the pan, invert onto a serving plate, and carefully remove the bottom of the pan and foil. Drizzle with chocolate glaze, spreading evenly on the top and sides.
Chocolate Glaze:
1. In a small mixing bowl, combine powdered sugar and vanilla extract; set aside.
2. In a small microwave-safe bowl, place butter, water, and baking chocolate. Microwave on HIGH (100%) power for 1 minute or until smooth when stirred. Add to the sugar mixture and beat until smooth. Add hot water, 1/2 tsp at a time, if needed, until the desired consistency is reached.
